description.content: Gets the data for the datasource. There are optional query parameters to filter the data

query.description: A series of query parameter used to filter the data for a datasource. This represents the entities for the datasource. E.g.: entityCodes=ACCOUNT&entityCodes=FUNDS
A series of query parameter used to filter the data for a datasource. This is the entity key value for an entity selection. E.g.: entityKeys=1&entityKeys=Test2
Non-negative maximum number of entries to return. Default is 25
Non-negative number of entries to skip. Default is 0

item.name: Factset Gets a List of Report Instances

request.name: Factset Gets a List of Report Instances

description.content: Gets a list of report instances. This can be filtered down further by including query parameters in the URL. For example, a report definition id can be added so the only report instances returned are the ones with a matching id

query.description: Acts as a filter for the retrieval process. Filters the report instances that match the report definition code
A series of query parameters used to filter the report instances by entity code. E.g: entityCode=DATE&entityCode=SYSTEM_LANG
A series of query parameters used to filter the report instances by entity keys. E.g: entityKey=en-gb
Acts as a filter for the retrieval process. Filters the report instances that match the section filter
Acts as a filter for the retrieval process. Filters the report instances that match the output format
The column to sort on. Append - to sort in descending order. If parameter is not given, sorting will be based on the report instance id in descending order
Non-negative maximum number of entries to return
Non-negative number of entries to skip

response.name: Successful operation

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

response.name: Unauthorised

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

response.name: No permissions to view reports in VRS

item.name: Factset Gets a List of Report Instances

request.name: Factset Gets a List of Report Instances

description.content: Gets a list of report instances. This can be filtered down further by including query parameters in the URL. For example, a report definition id can be added so the only report instances returned are the ones with a matching id

query.description: Acts as a filter for the retrieval process. Filters the report instances that match the report definition code
A series of query parameters used to filter the report instances by entity code. E.g: entityCode=DATE&entityCode=SYSTEM_LANG
A series of query parameters used to filter the report instances by entity keys. E.g: entityKey=en-gb
Acts as a filter for the retrieval process. Filters the report instances that match the section filter
Acts as a filter for the retrieval process. Filters the report instances that match the output format
The column to sort on. Append - to sort in descending order. If parameter is not given, sorting will be based on the report instance id in descending order
Non-negative maximum number of entries to return
Non-negative number of entries to skip

response.name: Successful operation

response.name: Unauthorised

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

A series of query parameter used to filter the data for a datasource. This is the entity key value for an entity selection. E.g.: entityKeys=1&entityKeys=Test2

Non-negative number of entries to skip. Default is 0

}

item.name: Factset Gets a List of Report Instances

request.name: Factset Gets a List of Report Instances

description.content: Gets a list of report instances. This can be filtered down further by including query parameters in the URL. For example, a report definition id can be added so the only report instances returned are the ones with a matching id

query.description: Acts as a filter for the retrieval process. Filters the report instances that match the report definition code
A series of query parameters used to filter the report instances by entity code. E.g: entityCode=DATE&entityCode=SYSTEM_LANG
A series of query parameters used to filter the report instances by entity keys. E.g: entityKey=en-gb
Acts as a filter for the retrieval process. Filters the report instances that match the section filter
Acts as a filter for the retrieval process. Filters the report instances that match the output format
The column to sort on. Append - to sort in descending order. If parameter is not given, sorting will be based on the report instance id in descending order
Non-negative maximum number of entries to return
Non-negative number of entries to skip

Non-negative number of entries to skip. Default is 0
Whether to show all field values for each entity row. Value should either be 1 or 0. Default is 0 (false)

The Vermilion API, enables users to programmatically access FactSet's
Vermilion Reporting Suite (VRS) and seamlessly integrate into customer's
3rd party applications. The API is referred to as two key product
initiatives, the REST API and the SCIM API.

Contact Support:
 Name: FactSet Research Systems
 Email: api@factset.com
